title: Office - Late to Meeting
question_text: You are 5 minutes late to a meeting. What do you say?
opt1: Sorry I'm late. Traffic was bad.
explanation: Apologize briefly and give a short reason. Don't make excuses or blame others.

title: Office - Asking for Vacation
question_text: How do you request time off from your manager?
opt1: Could I request a few days off next month?
explanation: Politely request using 'Could I' or 'May I'. Give advance notice.

title: Office - Computer Problem
question_text: Your computer won't turn on. Who do you call?
opt1: IT support.
explanation: IT support handles technology problems. Most companies have a help desk number or ticket system.

title: Office - Answering Phone
question_text: You answer a call at work. What do you say?
opt1: Good morning ABC Company. This is Maria speaking.
explanation: State the company name and your name. It sounds professional and helps the caller know they dialed correctly.

title: Factory - Safety First
question_text: You see a coworker not wearing safety goggles. What do you say?
opt1: Hey don't forget your safety goggles. It's the rule.
opt2: I don't care what you do.
explanation: Politely remind them of safety rules. It protects everyone, including you.

title: Factory - Machine Problem
question_text: A machine starts making a strange noise. What do you do?
opt1: Stop the machine and tell your supervisor.
explanation: Safety first. Stop the machine and report it. Never fix machinery unless you're trained.

title: Factory - Heavy Lifting
question_text: A box is too heavy for you to lift alone. What do you say?
opt1: Could you help me lift this? It's too heavy alone.
explanation: Ask for help directly. Lifting alone can cause injury. Team lifting is safer.

title: School - Late to Class
question_text: You arrive 10 minutes late to class. What do you do?
opt1: Quietly enter and sit down. Apologize to the teacher after class.
opt2: Walk in loudly and announce you're here.
explanation: Enter quietly to avoid disturbing other students. Apologize after class, not during the lesson.

title: School - Forgot Homework
question_text: You forgot your homework. What do you say to the teacher?
opt1: I'm sorry. I forgot my homework. Can I bring it tomorrow?
opt2: I did it but I lost it.
explanation: Be honest and take responsibility. Offer a solution (bring it tomorrow). Lying makes it worse.

title: School - Group Project
question_text: A group member isn't doing their share of the work. What do you say to them?
opt1: Can we divide the tasks more clearly? I want to make sure everyone knows their part.
explanation: Address the problem directly but politely. Focus on solutions, not blame.

title: School - Asking for Help
question_text: You don't understand the math lesson. What do you say to the teacher?
opt1: Could you explain that again? I didn't fully understand.
explanation: Politely ask for clarification. Most teachers are happy to help students who ask.

title: Construction - PPE
question_text: What does PPE stand for?
opt1: Personal Protective Equipment.
explanation: PPE = hard hat, safety glasses, high-vis vest, gloves, steel-toe boots, hearing protection.

title: Construction - Fall Protection
question_text: At what height is fall protection required?
opt1: Usually 6 feet (2 meters) or higher.
explanation: OSHA (US) requires fall protection at 6 feet. Construction is one of the most dangerous jobs because of falls.

title: Park - Leave No Trace
question_text: What does 'leave no trace' mean?
opt1: Take all your trash with you. Leave the park as clean as you found it.
opt3: Carve your name into a tree.
explanation: The principle is: don't leave any evidence you were there. Pack it in, pack it out.

title: Bicycle - Hand Signals
question_text: What does your left arm straight out to the side mean?
opt1: I am turning left.
explanation: Hand signals tell drivers what you plan to do. Left arm straight out = left turn. Left arm bent up = right turn. Left arm bent down = stop.

title: Bicycle - Right of Way
question_text: Who goes first at a 4-way stop when you're on a bike?
opt1: The person who arrived first

title: Cooking - Knife Safety
question_text: What should you never do with a knife?
opt1: Catch a falling knife.
explanation: A falling knife has no handle. Let it fall. Moving your hand to catch it is how people get severe cuts.

title: Cooking - Smoke Point
question_text: What happens when oil reaches its 'smoke point'?
opt1: The oil starts smoking
explanation: The oil doubles in volume.

title: Cooking - Food Safety
question_text: What is the 'danger zone' temperature for food?
opt1: Between 40°F and 140°F (4°C - 60°C). Bacteria grows fastest here.
explanation: Don't leave cooked food at room temperature for more than 2 hours. In hot weather, 1 hour. Refrigerate leftovers quickly.

title: Restaurant - Server Mistake
question_text: A server brings you the wrong dish. What do you say?

explanation: Politely state the error and what you ordered. Don't be rude. Mistakes happen.

title: Restaurant - Allergy
question_text: You have a severe nut allergy. What do you tell the server?
opt1: I have a severe nut allergy. Can you tell the chef?
opt3: I'll die if there are nuts.
opt4: Don't put nuts in my food.
explanation: State the allergy clearly and ask them to tell the chef directly. Some kitchens have allergy protocols.

title: Restaurant - Tipping
question_text: In the US what is the standard tip at a sit-down restaurant?
opt1: 15-20% of the pre-tax bill.
explanation: Servers in the US rely on tips because their base wage is low (often $2-5/hour). 18-20% is now common for good service.

title: LIE DOWN
definition:
\bDEITAR-SE\b\br
To assume a reclining position.\br
Everytime my dad lies down to read, he falls asleep.
